Assignment For Session 2:
The Nightmare Story


1. Create your own Nightmare Story using the formula below:

The Nightmare Story
The Transition
The Intro
The Bad Part
The Really Bad Part (Focal Point)
Declaration of Independence
The Moral: Your Journey To Freedom

Part 1: The Transition
A simple transition phase to intro into your story.
(Example: I wasn't always the (your high-level achievement/credential here) you see today…)

Your Transition

WRITE IT HERE:



Part 2: The Intro
This is a detailed account of the setting in which your nightmare story takes place. To optimize the details in order to create a powerful narrative, answer the following questions. Feel free to be as detailed as you like.

(See slide PDF for example of my entire nightmare story…)

Answer The Following:

WHEN? (Date/Day/Time):


WEATHER?


WHAT WERE YOU WEARING?


WHO WERE YOU WITH?


WHERE WERE YOU?


WHAT WAS YOUR EMOTIONAL STATE?



Part 3: The Bad Part
1. You are building rapport at this point by first saying something positive within a negative phrase.
(Example: I thought things could never get worse… and knew I had to make a change, deep down inside… Just like you know you desire a change, because like me, you truly deserve better… )

2. You build in the "one more thing" element:
(Example: Yes, I knew something had to change, but one more thing had to happen in order to push me over the edge…)

Your Bad Part:

WRITE IT HERE:


Part 4: The Really Bad Part
1. Intro into your Focal Point. This is the epicenter of your big event.
(Example: Then I pulled into the shopping mall with my cousin Lisa.) 

2. The transition phrase:
(Example: And then, the worst thing happened:)

3. Describe the event in detail. (See PDF Slides for a detailed Really Bad Part event.)  This is just the story of what actually took place.

4. How did you feel afterwards? Alone, confused, scared, pissed off, angry, determined, etc.

Your Really Bad Part:

WRITE IT HERE:


Part 5: The Declaration of Independence
1. "I made a…"
(Example:  Vow, promise, oath to myself, line in the sand, etc.) 

2. Describe your statement:
(Example: I made a promise to myself at that very moment to do whatever it took to solve __________... or die trying!)

Your Declaration of Independence:

WRITE IT HERE:

Part 6: The Moral: Your Journey To Freedom
1. Your dedication and result of your Declaration
(Example:  I read over 400 books on the subject of ______. I went back to school and actually got my Masters Degree…) 

2. The RESULT of this dedication:
(Example: Through it all, I uncovered a pattern…) Or: Secret, formula, roadmap, blueprint, etc.

3. Final Transition:
(Example: And today, in just a few minutes, I'm going to share it with you.) 

Your Journey To Freedom:

WRITE IT HERE:

Nightmare Story Notes

Do not be afraid of length.
People bond and deepen rapport through stories. Feel free to write a fairly long one.

2. You can create your story out-of-sequence, or with a combination of characters and events forming one story.
Just do not overtly lie or mislead.
